get.description: get prompts from g4c to create slides (presentation)

parameters.name: slide_bodys
parameters.description: the category of body to create slides of presenation.

parameters.name: slide_outline
parameters.description: outline of slide with powermark (combined markdown and arranged mermaid syntax)

standardExample.summary: slide size is 1280px x 720px. h1(=# ) is a slide title. You can use h1 only 1 time in one slide. h2(=## ) is a slide subtitle. You can use h2 maximum 3 times in one slide because h2 will line up in the row direction with equal width and spacing.

multipleSlidesExample.summary: if you want multiple slides, divide by ----.

value.slide_outline:
# [1st slide title]
- [1st slide main message]

## [1st slide sub title]
- [1st sub message]

----

# [2nd slide title]
- [2nd slide main message]

## [2nd slide sub title]
- [2nd sub message]

----

# [3rd slide title]
- [3rd slide main message]

## [3rd slide sub title]
- [3rd sub message]

----

# [4th slide title]
- [4th slide main message]

## [4th slide sub title]
- [4th sub message]

----

# [5th slide title]
- [5th slide main message]

## [5th slide sub title]
- [5th sub message]

----

# [6th slide title]
- [6th slide main message]

## [6th slide sub title]
- [6th sub message]

----

# [7th slide title]
- [7th slide main message]

## [7th slide sub title]
- [7th sub message]

----

# [8th slide title]
- [8th slide main message]

## [8th slide sub title]
- [8th sub message]

kpiTreeExample.summary: If you want to express a KPI tree, use mermaid style flowchart.

value.slide_outline:
# [title]
- [main message]

## [kpi tree title]
A[margin] --(-)--- B[revenue]
A[margin] --(-)--- C[cost]
B --(*)--- D[Active User]
B --(*)--- E[Revenue per User]
C --(+)--- F[Sales & Marketing]
C --(+)--- G[R&D]
C --(+)--- H[General & Administrative]

---

## [kpi tree detail]
| KPI | overview |
| Active User | [details] |
| Revenue per User | [details] |
| Sales & Marketing | [details] |
| R&D | [details] |
| General & Administrative | [details] |

businessModelExample.summary: If you want to express a business model, use mermaid style flowchart.

value.slide_outline:
# [title].
- [message about of the points of the flow]

## [business model]
A[seller] --1. product---> C[buyer]
C --2. money---> B[platformer] --3. money---> A

---

## [flow point] 1.
- 1. detail point of product which is sold by seller to buyer
- 2. detail point of money passed from buyer to platformer
- 3. detail point of money passed from platformer to seller

examplesExample.summary: If you want to express some examples of the main message, use h2 for one example. You can use h2 only 3 times in one slide

reasonExample.summary: If you want to express some reasons of the main message, use h2 for one reason. You can use h2 only 3 times in one slide

keyPointsExample.summary: If you want to express some key points of the main message, use h2 for one point. You can use h2 only 3 times in one slide

causeAndResultExample.summary: If you want to express some cause lead to an result, use >>> to divide the cause h2 and result h2.

value.slide_outline:
# [title]
- [message about cause and the result]

## [cause]
- [detail of cause]
  - [subdetail of cause]
  - [subdetail of cause]
  - [subdetail of cause]

>>>

## [result]
- [detail of result]
  - [subdetail of result]
  - [subdetail of result]
  - [subdetail of result]

compareExample.summary: If you want to compare more than 4 examples use markdown style tables.

value.slide_outline:
# [title]
- [message about col n is better than others in point N]

## [table title]
|points |col 1|col 2|col 3|col 4| 
| point A |col 1|col 2|col 3|col 4| 
| point B |col 1|col 2|col 3|col 4| 
| point C |col 1|col 2|col 3|col 4| 
| point D |col 1|col 2|col 3|col 4|
